attacking_style: Breakaway specialist targeting major summit finishes, particularly on Grand Tour mountain stages. Attacks from long-range moves rather than late explosive accelerations, typically establishing position early in climbs.
team_tactics: Relies on breakaway insertion rather than team-led setups; minimal domestique support visible, suggesting autonomous riding role focused on opportunistic long-distance moves.
terrain_preference: High-altitude summit finishes on Grand Tour stages where breakaways can succeed. Strong on sustained climbs (8+ km) that favor steady tempo over explosive accelerations. Volcanic terrain (Etna) proven effective.
vulnerability: Lacks repeatability when initial attacks are neutralized; single-punch climbing profile limits ability to respond to multiple accelerations or counter-attacks in finale scenarios.
key_patterns: Targets breakaway opportunities on major Grand Tour mountain stages (2 observations)
Succeeds on long summit finishes requiring sustained climbing power over explosiveness (2 observations)
Vulnerable when caught after timed attacks, limited response capacity (2 observations)
Performs above reputation level in underdog breakaway scenarios at Grand Tours (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Vuelta showed tactical awareness with flamme rouge timing, but revealed same single-acceleration limitation seen in 2020, suggesting consistent rather than improving capabilities
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 11
